hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'creat' — 'crea' is a command form
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive present would be 'creet' — 'crea' is a direct imperative
hints.Infinitive: The infinitive would be 'creare' — 'crea' is 2nd person singular imperative

hints.credo, credere, credidi, creditum: These are the principal parts of 'credere' (to believe), a 3rd conjugation verb
hints.cresco, crescere, crevi, cretum: These are the principal parts of 'crescere' (to grow), a 3rd conjugation verb
hints.cerno, cernere, crevi, cretum: These are the principal parts of 'cernere' (to perceive/decide)

hints.Nominative: 'Cor' can be nominative, but here it's the direct object of 'crea' — create a heart

hints.2nd: 2nd declension neuter nouns end in -um — 'cor' with genitive 'cordis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'cor' with genitive 'cordis' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'cor' is a 3rd declension neuter noun

hints.Nominative: Nominative neuter would also be 'mundum', but it agrees with 'Cor' as accusative object

hints.Accusative: 'in' + accusative means motion toward — here 'in me' means 'within me' (location)

hints.Nominative: Nominative of the 1st person pronoun is 'ego'
hints.Accusative: 'me' can be accusative, but governed by 'in' for location, it's ablative here
hints.Dative: Dative of the 1st person pronoun is 'mihi'

translation: Create a clean heart in me
